text: The principal quantum number n determines:
options: The orientation of the orbital in space
The energy level and average distance of the electron from the nucleus
explanation: Principal quantum number n (1, 2, 3...): determines the primary energy level (shell) and average electron-nucleus distance (r ∝ n² in Bohr model). n also sets the range of allowed l values (l = 0 to n−1) and thus the number of subshells and orbitals in each shell: n² orbitals per shell, 2n² electrons maximum.

text: Which type of emission line spectrum results from electrons falling from higher to lower energy levels?
options: Emission spectrum (bright lines at specific wavelengths corresponding to ΔE = hf)
explanation: Emission spectrum: when electrons fall from higher to lower energy levels, they emit photons with E = hf = ΔE. Each element has a unique set of energy levels → unique emission spectrum (fingerprint). For hydrogen: Lyman series (to n=1, UV), Balmer series (to n=2, visible), Paschen series (to n=3, IR).

text: The root-mean-square (rms) speed of gas molecules is given by:
options: v_rms = √(3RT/M) where M is molar mass in kg/mol
v_rms = √(kT/m) where k is the spring constant
explanation: v_rms = √(3RT/M), where R = 8.314 J/(mol·K), T = absolute temperature, M = molar mass in kg/mol. Average KE per molecule = ½mv_rms² = 3/2 kT (kinetic theory). At 300K, v_rms(H₂) = √(3×8.314×300/0.002) ≈ 1934 m/s; v_rms(O₂) = √(3×8.314×300/0.032) ≈ 484 m/s. Lighter gases move faster.

text: A ball on a string moves in a vertical circle. At the top of the circle, the minimum speed required to maintain circular motion is:
options: v = 0 (gravity always provides enough centripetal force)
v = √(rg), where the centripetal force is provided entirely by gravity (T = 0 at minimum speed)
explanation: At the top of a vertical circle, both gravity and tension point downward (toward center). At minimum speed, tension T = 0 and gravity alone provides centripetal force: mg = mv²/r → v_min = √(rg). If v < √(rg), the string would need to push (tension becomes negative), which a string can't do — it would go slack and the ball leaves circular motion.

text: Which of the following correctly applies Newton's Third Law?
options: A book on a table is in equilibrium because the table's normal force equals the book's weight (these are NOT Newton's 3rd Law pairs)
The friction force from the road on a car's tires (forward) and the friction force from the car's tires on the road (backward) are Newton's 3rd Law pairs
Gravitational force on a planet and centripetal force on the planet are Newton's 3rd Law pairs
The normal force and gravity on a book are Newton's 3rd Law pairs
explanation: Newton's 3rd Law: for every action, there is an equal and opposite reaction. The pairs must be: same type of force, between the same two objects, in opposite directions. The road exerts forward friction on the tire (what moves the car); the tire exerts backward friction on the road. These are a valid 3rd Law pair. The normal force and gravity on the book are NOT a 3rd Law pair — they're both on the same object and are different types of forces.

text: A block is pushed up a frictionless incline of angle θ with constant force F parallel to the incline. The work done by gravity over displacement d along the incline is:
options: W = −mgd sinθ (negative — gravity opposes uphill motion)
W = 0 (gravity is perpendicular to incline surface)
explanation: Gravity acts downward (magnitude mg). Displacement is along the incline (at angle θ to horizontal). Gravity component along the incline (opposing motion) = mg sinθ. Work by gravity: W_grav = −(mg sinθ)(d) = −mgd sinθ. Negative because force component (mg sinθ, down the incline) opposes displacement (up the incline). Equivalently: W_grav = −ΔPE = −mgh = −mg(d sinθ).

text: A wave has speed v = 340 m/s and frequency f = 1000 Hz. Its wavelength and period are:
explanation: λ = v/f = 340/1000 = 0.34 m. T = 1/f = 1/1000 = 0.001 s = 1 ms. These represent a 1000 Hz (1 kHz) sound wave in air. The wave travels 0.34 m (34 cm) per cycle and completes 1000 cycles per second.

text: Power transmitted by a wave is proportional to:
explanation: Wave power (energy per unit time) is proportional to the square of the amplitude: P ∝ A². This applies to all wave types (mechanical, EM). Intensity (power per unit area) I ∝ A². For a sound wave, doubling amplitude quadruples intensity (increases by 6 dB). For a photon: E = hf (one photon's energy); for many photons, total intensity ∝ n × E ∝ amplitude².

text: The decibel (dB) sound intensity level scale is logarithmic. A 10 dB increase corresponds to:
explanation: β = 10 log(I/I₀), where I₀ = 10⁻¹² W/m² (threshold of hearing). Δβ = 10 log(I₂/I₁). If Δβ = 10 dB: 10 = 10 log(I₂/I₁) → log(I₂/I₁) = 1 → I₂/I₁ = 10. Every 10 dB = 10× intensity. Every 3 dB ≈ 2× intensity (since log₁₀(2) ≈ 0.3). A conversation at 60 dB is 10⁶× more intense than threshold.

text: Two equal charges of +q are placed 1 m apart. The electric force on each charge is F. If the distance is reduced to 0.5 m, the new force is:
explanation: Coulomb's Law: F = kq²/r². If r → r/2: F_new = kq²/(r/2)² = kq²/(r²/4) = 4kq²/r² = 4F. Halving the distance quadruples the force. The inverse square law means electrostatic (and gravitational) forces are extremely sensitive to distance.

text: A proton (charge +e) is placed in a uniform electric field E = 500 N/C. Its acceleration is (m_proton = 1.67 × 10⁻²⁷ kg, e = 1.6 × 10⁻¹⁹ C):
explanation: F = qE = (1.6×10⁻¹⁹)(500) = 8×10⁻¹⁷ N. a = F/m = 8×10⁻¹⁷/1.67×10⁻²⁷ ≈ 4.8×10¹⁰ m/s². Protons (small mass, fundamental charge) accelerate enormously in electric fields. Electrons (1836× lighter) accelerate even faster in the same field.

text: In a series RLC circuit at resonance:
options: The capacitive and inductive reactances cancel (X_L = X_C), impedance = R (minimum), current is maximum
All voltage appears across the resistor only
explanation: At resonance in a series RLC circuit: X_L = ωL = 1/(ωC) = X_C → impedances cancel → Z = R (minimum impedance). Maximum current I = V/R. Resonant frequency: ω₀ = 1/√(LC), f₀ = 1/(2π√(LC)). Applications: radio tuning, MRI (Larmor frequency matching), filters.

text: Kirchhoff's Current Law (KCL) states:
options: The sum of voltages around any closed loop = 0
The sum of currents entering a node equals the sum of currents leaving (ΣI_in = ΣI_out)
Current is the same everywhere in a series circuit
Resistance divides proportionally among parallel resistors
explanation: KCL (junction rule): at any node (junction), ΣI_in = ΣI_out (conservation of charge). Kirchhoff's Voltage Law (KVL): around any closed loop, ΣΔV = 0 (conservation of energy). These two laws, combined with Ohm's Law, allow complete circuit analysis.

text: A transformer has primary voltage 240 V, secondary voltage 12 V. The turns ratio N_p/N_s is:
explanation: V_p/V_s = N_p/N_s. 240/12 = 20. N_p/N_s = 20 (step-down transformer: primary has 20× more turns than secondary, reduces voltage by 20). Secondary current is proportionally larger: I_s = I_p × (N_p/N_s) = 20 × I_p (more current at lower voltage to conserve power P = VI).

text: The displacement current (Ampere-Maxwell Law extension) is associated with:
options: Current through a capacitor's dielectric gap (no actual charge movement, but changing electric field produces a magnetic field as if current flowed)
explanation: Maxwell added a displacement current term to Ampere's Law: J_d = ε₀(∂E/∂t). A changing electric field (e.g., between capacitor plates as capacitor charges) creates a magnetic field, exactly as a real current would. This completed the symmetry of Maxwell's equations and predicts electromagnetic waves traveling at speed c = 1/√(ε₀μ₀).

text: An inductor L stores energy in its magnetic field when current I flows through it. The stored energy is:
explanation: Inductor energy: U = ½LI², where L = inductance (henries, H) and I = current (amperes). Compare to capacitor: U = ½CV². Inductors oppose changes in current (V = −L dI/dt) and store energy in magnetic fields; capacitors oppose changes in voltage and store energy in electric fields.

text: Which property of water is most directly responsible for the high heat capacity of oceans and moderation of coastal climates?
options: Water's high specific heat (4.18 J/g°C) due to extensive hydrogen bonding
explanation: Water's high specific heat (4.18 J/g°C, far higher than metals or air) means oceans can absorb vast amounts of solar energy with minimal temperature change, and release that energy slowly in winter. This moderates coastal climate (maritime climate: less extreme temperature swings than continental interiors). Both the absorption and release involve disrupting and forming hydrogen bonds.

text: A 2 μF capacitor is charged to 100 V. The charge stored is:
Two capacitors C₁ = 4 μF and C₂ = 4 μF connected in series. Their equivalent capacitance is:
explanation: Capacitors in series: 1/C_eq = 1/C₁ + 1/C₂ = 1/4 + 1/4 = 2/4 → C_eq = 2 μF. For two equal capacitors in series: C_eq = C/2. Note: for capacitors, series = smaller capacitance (like parallel for resistors); parallel = larger capacitance (like series for resistors). This is opposite to resistors.

text: The work done by an external agent to move charge Q through a potential difference ΔV is:
explanation: Work done to move charge: W = QΔV. This is equivalent to saying that potential difference is defined as V = W/Q (work per unit charge). When an electron (q = −e) moves through ΔV = +1 V (against the field), the work done ON the electron by external agent = (−e)(+1 V) = −1.6×10⁻¹⁹ J (field does negative work, external agent does positive work).

text: Which quantum numbers specify an electron in the 3d subshell?
explanation: The principal quantum number n=3 designates the third shell, and the azimuthal quantum number l=2 designates a d subshell (l: 0=s, 1=p, 2=d, 3=f).

text: Which element has the highest first ionization energy?
explanation: Helium has the highest first ionization energy of all elements (~2372 kJ/mol) due to its small atomic radius and full 1s² shell with no shielding electrons.

text: Across a period from left to right, atomic radius generally:
options: Increases due to more electron shells
Increases due to decreasing nuclear charge
Decreases due to increasing nuclear charge with same shielding
explanation: Across a period, nuclear charge increases while electrons are added to the same shell (minimal additional shielding), causing the electron cloud to contract.

text: The electron configuration of Fe²⁺ is:
explanation: Neutral Fe is [Ar] 3d⁶ 4s². When Fe loses 2 electrons to form Fe²⁺, the 4s electrons are removed first, leaving [Ar] 3d⁶.

text: Which property generally increases going down a group in the periodic table?
explanation: Atomic radius increases down a group because each successive element has an additional electron shell, increasing the distance between the nucleus and valence electrons.
